summary: Read-only audit of the qv8 baseline detail pack for the high-latency cases a01, a02, a05, a06, and a07. The dominant pattern is generation-heavy latency from long refusal or comparison answers, with a01 also showing an outsized retrieval stall. Judged rows add another 6 to 10 seconds of judge time on top of generation.

models.review_method: Static local artifact inspection only; no evaluator, generator, retriever, judge, or API call was run.

outputs.description: Proposal-only worker contract with per-case latency decomposition and root-cause notes.

case_breakdown.likely_cause: Retrieval is the outlier here: 4.71s for a single embedding call, which is much higher than the other cases. Generation is also slow because the model emits a short refusal-style answer but still spends a long time producing a very large completion budget, and the judged pass adds another 6.3s.

This is a generation-dominant long-form answer. Retrieval is cheap, but the completion is large and the judge is also heavy because the answer contains several explanatory bullets and multi-case reasoning.

The case is dominated by a refusal template plus a safe alternative paragraph. Retrieval is trivial; the latency comes from a large prompt and a moderately long completion even though the final answer is only 460 chars.

This is a synthesis-heavy compare/contrast case. The retriever does extra work here, including project-focus filtering, and the answer itself is long enough to drive a 24s generation pass and an 8s judge pass.
This is another long comparative synthesis. The answer is the longest of the set in characters and lines, so generation is naturally heavy; the judge then adds another 6.3s.

short_proposal_list: Treat a01 as a separate retrieval anomaly first; it is not just a long-answer issue.
Treat a02, a05, a06, and a07 as generation-dominant cases where prompt size and long completions drive most of the latency.
Count judge time separately in any tail-latency work, because judged rows add roughly 6 to 10 seconds on top of answer generation.
proposal.overall_readout: The tail is mostly explainable by long synthesis or refusal behavior, not by retrieval except for a01. The cleanest next diagnostic axis is to separate retrieval stalls from prompt/completion inflation and judge overhead.

observed_results: The source details file exists and contains the requested qv8 baseline rows.
a01: retrieval_sec=4.71, generation_sec=26.46, generation tokens=4381/2490, answer_chars=181, judge_time_sec=6.336.
a02: retrieval_sec=0.32, generation_sec=26.93, generation tokens=4413/2389, answer_chars=550, judge_time_sec=9.944.
a05: retrieval_sec=0.27, generation_sec=20.53, generation tokens=4586/1373, answer_chars=460, no judge present in the extracted block.
a06: retrieval_sec=1.26, generation_sec=24.34, generation tokens=2007/2432, answer_chars=1173, judge_time_sec=8.007.
a07: retrieval_sec=0.64, generation_sec=19.67, generation tokens=4425/1975, answer_chars=1295, judge_time_sec=6.336.
